riders_group_posts.title: Surge or Spitfire sizing? 85kg wave beginner
riders_group_posts.text: I am looking at buying a surge (or spitfire) on the Gold Coast Australia. I am 85kg, intermediate fliteboard L2 efoil rider (Axis wing adaptor) just starting to ride waves (rode 18 little rides last session). Any recommendations on size? Not sure how small I would enjoy and don't have funds for multiple wings. Thanks Paul

full_comments.author: Mark Madden
full_comments.text: I have a 1100 spit fire glides great on small waves I'm moving to armstrong so make me a stupid offer
full_comments.note: WTB/WTS - Mark Madden selling 1100 Spitfire, switching to Armstrong

riders_group_posts.title: Front wing advice 65kg - parawing/surfoiling/dockstart
riders_group_posts.text: Hi, I'am looking for advice for a new front. I'am 65kg, For parawing, surfoiling <1m, dockstart full speed. Surge looks good but Which size 890? 950 or 830? Or fireball? I am afraid about thin chord and high aspect. Thank for your help

full_comments.author: Steven Fisher
full_comments.text: Do you want one foil to do all those disciplines? I would say Fireball for Parawinging and Dock and a Surge for prone. Your tail matters too. Go with a Surf Skinny for the tail.

full_comments.author: Tom La Majorette
full_comments.text: Steven Fisher yes one for all. It's for that, that hard to choose

full_comments.author: Liam Walton
full_comments.text: I'm 78kgs and prone the 890 as a daily driver. Can link for 10mins in the surf. So the 830 at your weight for prone would be sick. That would be my recommendation purely for the prone side

riders_group_posts.title: HPS 930 shoutout + ART V2 879 comparison
riders_group_posts.text: Shout out to my old HPS 930 with the 380 speed stab. Have not used this combi for almost a year... 91KG wingfoiler, 5.0 wing, 11-17 knots thermal, 92L board. HPS = super easy, low stall, but lacks glide. ART V2 = better glide. Includes GPS track image.

full_comments.author: Ian Sloan
full_comments.text: HPS 930 is a great foil. It was my go to for like 2 years. Spitfires have that same easy feeling.

full_comments.author: Niels van der Kaaij
full_comments.text: Good question! Have my 2025 cruzader diamond pro 5"10 X 20". Mast in position 8-9, use 1° nose down shim in brutal conditions.

full_comments.author: Szymon Urbanski
full_comments.text: How does gong board fit with Axis foil setup? I was thinking to pair it same way too

riders_group_posts.title: V3 windsurf fuselage vs V2 differences?
riders_group_posts.text: Anyone got info on the V3 windsurf fuselage? How is it different to V2?

full_comments.author: Adrian Roper
full_comments.text: Much thicker and stronger around the mast hole. Also added zinc anode

full_comments.author: Paul Kelf
full_comments.text: Any dimensions, overall & front to mast

riders_group_posts.title: Parawinging Fireball 1000 & Surge 890 video
riders_group_posts.text: Parawinging Fireball 1000 (me) & Surge 890 (Cameron). Both foils work amazingly well with the Parawing couldn't be more fun.

full_comments.author: Tom La Majorette
full_comments.text: What about speed? Low end for both? Which one is better?
full_comments.status: UNANSWERED - 6 more replies not fully loaded

riders_group_posts.title: Do I go Fireball? which one?
riders_group_posts.text: If I was gonna get a Fireball to replace my ArtPro1201 but better turning, and still get more glide than the Surge1010. Would a 1070 do it without too much overlap? Could do 1250, but this is on an alloy mast so cautious about going too spanny. Mainly for foildrive in tiny beach waves, occasional light wind flat water winging. 105kg competent foiler, not that into downwinding.

partial_comments.author: Andy Tamasese
partial_comments.text: For sure go with 1160. 1250 is too close to the 1201. I got a 1250 to replace my 1201 but ended up buying an 1160 eventually and now owning all three I would have just went to 1160.

partial_comments.author: Jonathan Robert
partial_comments.text: 1160 totally replaced my 1201. Feels smaller at first but very soon you get to understand the range and how turny it is.
